steps.title: Identify the surface area components
steps.content:
A right rectangular pyramid's surface area equals its rectangular base plus four triangular faces.

The base area is:
$$\text{Base} = l \times w = 18 \times 9 = 162 \text{ square units}$$
Since the base is rectangular (not square), there are two types of triangular faces: two faces with base 18 and two with base 9. Each type has a different slant height, so we must find both.

steps.title: Calculate the two distinct slant heights using the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
In a right pyramid, the apex sits directly above the center of the base. The slant height of each triangular face is the distance from the apex to the midpoint of a base edge.

For faces with base 18 (the length edges):
The midpoint is $w/2 = 4.5$ units from the base center.
$$s_1 = \sqrt{\left(\frac{w}{2}\right)^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{4.5^2 + 12^2} = \sqrt{20.25 + 144} = \sqrt{164.25}$$
Simplify: $164.25 = \frac{657}{4} = \frac{9 \times 73}{4}$, so $\sqrt{164.25} = \frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2}$

For faces with base 9 (the width edges):
The midpoint is $l/2 = 9$ units from the base center.
$$s_2 = \sqrt{\left(\frac{l}{2}\right)^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{9^2 + 12^2} = \sqrt{81 + 144} = \sqrt{225} = 15$$

steps.title: Calculate all face areas and sum to find total surface area
steps.content:
Two triangular faces with base 18 and slant height $\frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2}$:
$$A_1 = 2 \times \frac{1}{2} \times 18 \times \frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2} = 27\sqrt{73}$$
Two triangular faces with base 9 and slant height 15:
$$A_2 = 2 \times \frac{1}{2} \times 9 \times 15 = 135 \text{ square units}$$
Sum all components:
$$\text{Surface Area} = 162 + 27\sqrt{73} + 135 = 297 + 27\sqrt{73}$$
Evaluate numerically: $\sqrt{73} \approx 8.544$, so $27\sqrt{73} \approx 230.69$
$$\text{Surface Area} \approx 297 + 230.69 = 527.69 \approx \mathbf{527.7} \text{ square units}$$
